narrator: The owner of Alaska.Ai: warm and analytical, curious-first, grounded and plainspoken, quietly amazed but never hyped
notes: Style: genuine wonder at a real capability (two machines finding one of the rarest whales by ear and by eye), curious-first and warm, turning sober and grounded at the honest limit on line eight, landing quiet and open on the closing question. No hype, no sourness. Pace: brisk, ~55-63s for 127 words. Emotion lives here in the notes, not in spoken tags. Contrast: energy spread 2-5, no two neighbors equal, peak at line five (for the first time), lowest sober beat at line eight (the turn) and line ten (the open button).
style_prompt:
Style: warm, analytical, curious-first. Genuine wonder at a real capability, two systems that find one of the rarest whales on Earth by ear and by eye at once. Lean forward with interest early, let the wonder peak when the two senses meet, then turn honest and grounded when watching is not saving, and land the closing question quietly and open, direct to camera. Never hyped, never sour.
Pace: BRISK and energetic, keep it moving like a sharp modern explainer. Short natural breaths only, no long pauses, do not drag. Vary the tone line to line so no two sentences sound the same.
Emphasis: lean on the key word in each line; let numbers land.

lines.text: [curious] Off Anchorage swims one of the rarest whales on Earth. It just got two watchers.
lines.why: Curious forward lean opens the piece; the flip lands on 'two watchers', the promise the whole video pays off.

lines.text: Noah switched on two A.I. systems to find Cook Inlet's belugas.
lines.why: Settle after the bright hook; even informative delivery names the who and why, dropping energy for contrast.

lines.text: One listens. A deep learning model reads the noisy inlet and finds a single beluga call.
lines.why: First watcher revealed; lift back up from the setup, emphasis on the verb that defines the robot ear.

lines.text: One looks. Guy-uh scans satellite photos from orbit for the white smudge of a whale.
lines.why: Second watcher, parallel construction; slightly lower than its twin so the pair does not flatten into a chant.

lines.text: Ear and eye, the same whale, at once, [short pause] for the first time.
lines.why: The wonder peak; a beat before 'for the first time' lets the stereo-sensing payoff land as the emotional high point.

lines.text: It matters because only about three thirty one of these belugas are left.
lines.why: Drop from the peak into sober; slow slightly on the number so how few remain registers.

lines.text: There were thirteen hundred in nineteen seventy nine. A drop of almost eighty percent.
lines.why: The decline slams; a punchier grim stat lifts against line five so the loss has weight, not monotone.

lines.text: But watching is not saving. Sharper eyes do not quiet the boats or refill the salmon these whales need.
lines.why: THE TURN; a quiet grounded drop after the loud eighty-percent slam makes the honest limit hit hardest.

lines.text: What the A.I. gives Alaska is one hard, open number. [short pause] Three thirty one.
lines.why: Firm resolve lifts back up; the pause sets 'Three thirty one' as a plain, keyed-in public fact.

lines.text: Whether that becomes a floor to defend, or just a countdown on record, no machine decides.
lines.why: The open question; quiet, direct to camera, landing on 'decides' so the human choice, not the machine, closes the piece.

assembled_prompt:
Read ONLY the transcript below aloud as speech. The lines above "Transcript:" are direction; never speak them.
# AUDIO PROFILE: The owner of Alaska.Ai, warm and analytical, curious-first, grounded and plainspoken, quietly amazed but never hyped.
### DIRECTOR'S NOTES
Style: warm, analytical, curious-first. Genuine wonder at a real capability, two systems that find one of the rarest whales on Earth by ear and by eye at once. Lean forward with interest early, let the wonder peak when the two senses meet, then turn honest and grounded when watching is not saving, and land the closing question quietly and open, direct to camera. Never hyped, never sour.
Pace: BRISK and energetic, keep it moving like a sharp modern explainer. Short natural breaths only, no long pauses, do not drag. Vary the tone line to line so no two sentences sound the same.
Emphasis: lean on the key word in each line; let numbers land.
Transcript:
[curious] Off Anchorage swims one of the rarest whales on Earth. It just got two watchers.
Noah switched on two A.I. systems to find Cook Inlet's belugas.
One listens. A deep learning model reads the noisy inlet and finds a single beluga call.
One looks. Guy-uh scans satellite photos from orbit for the white smudge of a whale.
Ear and eye, the same whale, at once, [short pause] for the first time.
It matters because only about three thirty one of these belugas are left.
There were thirteen hundred in nineteen seventy nine. A drop of almost eighty percent.
But watching is not saving. Sharper eyes do not quiet the boats or refill the salmon these whales need.
What the A.I. gives Alaska is one hard, open number. [short pause] Three thirty one.
Whether that becomes a floor to defend, or just a countdown on record, no machine decides.
